summary: I moved back to Olympia, Washington about three and a half years ago from LA to start a touring project. My family is based here, so it made sense to move my company and work from this great city. I really love being hunkered down here and appreciate the hometown love and community.
cues: moved back three and a half years ago

summary: Brad Schrandt was my private saxophone teacher when I started playing sax in fifth grade. His lessons really opened up a new layer of music for me during those formative years. I owe a lot to Brad for helping me develop my early musical foundation.

I learned guitar chords from Kenny Loggins' books when I was starting to teach myself guitar. Those chords helped me develop alternate ways of hearing music and contributed to my songwriting style. Kenny Loggins' materials were an important influence in my early guitar learning.

I initially went to the University of Michigan because my sister Riana was there studying musical theater, and I wanted to stay close to home. I enrolled in the Performing Arts Technology program and minored in jazz studies as a singer. Although it was a great experience, I felt a disconnect and eventually decided to transfer to USC to better pursue my music career.

Riana is my older sister who was studying musical theater at the University of Michigan when I was deciding on college. I wanted to stay close to her because I wasn't ready to be on my own yet. Her presence influenced my initial choice to attend Michigan.

Performing Arts Technology was the program I enrolled in at the University of Michigan. It included computer programming, sound design, and recording arts, which I thought would challenge me beyond just vocal performance. However, the curriculum required two years of lab training before studio work, which made me feel impatient and disconnected from my passion for performing and songwriting.

Geri Allen was an incredible pianist and my jazz instructor at Michigan. She opened my eyes to improvisation, especially as a singer, which was a big breakthrough for me. Her teaching expanded my musical canvas and deepened my love for jazz and performance.

I traveled with the University of Michigan jazz band to New York City for the International Association for Jazz Education conference. It was my first time in a major city outside Seattle, and I performed at open mics and jam sessions. This experience was pivotal, giving me motivation, connections, and a sense of urgency about my music career.

My first trip to New York City was with the University of Michigan jazz band for the IAJE conference. Performing there at open mics and jam sessions introduced me to talented musicians and energized my career ambitions. It was a major moment that made me question my path and inspired me to pursue music more seriously.

Ann Arbor is where the University of Michigan is located, and where I spent my first year of college. It’s a wonderful small town, but after visiting New York City for the IAJE conference, I felt the need to be in a bigger city to jumpstart my career. That contrast made me reconsider my choices and eventually transfer to USC.

I transferred to the University of Southern California in Los Angeles after realizing Michigan wasn’t the right fit for me. USC was the best decision I ever made and changed my life, allowing me to go all in on my dream of being a singer-songwriter. Being in LA gave me the momentum and environment I needed to pursue my music career seriously.

Brian Wilson of the Beach Boys was at a dinner where I was honored with the Brian Wilson songwriting scholarship during my time at USC. Sitting next to him and talking about songwriting was surreal and validating. It was a 'pinch me' moment that confirmed I might have what it takes to succeed in music.

The Beach Boys are legendary musicians, and Brian Wilson, a key member, was part of a dinner where I received a songwriting scholarship in his name. Their music and Brian Wilson’s work have been heroes to me, so being recognized in that context was incredibly meaningful. It felt like a milestone in my professional journey.

Lamont Dozier, the incredible songwriter, was also at the dinner where I received the Brian Wilson songwriting scholarship. Talking with him and Brian Wilson about songwriting was an unforgettable experience that gave me confidence and inspiration. It was a pivotal moment in my music career.

Los Angeles is where I moved after transferring from Michigan to USC. It was the perfect place to put together a band and start working professionally in the music scene. Being in LA gave me access to the industry and opportunities that I didn’t have before, and it really helped me go all in on my dream.

Hollywood is part of the Los Angeles area where I pursued my music career after transferring to USC. It’s a hub for the entertainment industry and provided a vibrant environment for me to grow as a musician. Being in Hollywood meant being close to many opportunities and influential people in music.

The Sunset Strip in Los Angeles is a famous area known for its music venues and nightlife. While the transcript doesn’t detail my specific experiences there, it’s part of the LA music scene that I engaged with after moving here. It’s a place where many musicians, including myself, have performed and networked.

The Troubadour is a legendary music venue on the Sunset Strip in LA. Although I didn’t mention specific shows there, it’s one of the iconic places in the LA music scene where many artists perform. Being in LA, venues like the Troubadour are part of the environment that shaped my professional music experience.

The Key Club was another well-known music venue on the Sunset Strip in Los Angeles. Like the Troubadour, it’s part of the LA music scene that I became involved with after moving here. These venues represent the vibrant live music culture that supported my growth as a professional musician.
